title: 9 Amazing Herbal Tinctures to Cure Dry Lips Naturally

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry lips because it helps to reduce inflammation and protect the skin from damage, which can help to soothe and heal dry, cracked lips.

Kaempferol is good for dry lips because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe, moisturize, and protect dry, chapped skin on the lips, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Tannins are good for dry lips because they help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from environmental stressors, keeping it hydrated and soft.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe and moisturize dry lips. This tincture is made from the flowers of the marigold plant, which is rich in antioxidants and fatty acids. When applied to dry lips, calendula tincture helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further dryness. Its anti-inflammatory properties also reduce redness and irritation, making it a gentle and effective solution for dry, chapped lips. Additionally, calendula tincture promotes the healing of cracked skin, allowing the skin to repair itself naturally. As a result, it can provide long-lasting relief from dry, uncomfortable lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with dry lips, you will need glycyrrhiza root, vodka, and a glass jar. First, start by preparing the glycyrrhiza root by cleaning it with water and gently drying it with a paper towel. Next, chop the root into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar with them, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Then, pour enough vodka into the jar to completely cover the root pieces, making sure that the glycyrrhiza root is fully submerged in the vodka. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for about 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every day or two to help the vodka extract the medicinal properties from the root. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the root pieces. This glycyrrhiza tincture can be used as is or diluted with water to help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for dry lips

Aloe-emodin is good for dry lips because it has strong soothing properties that help to calm and heal dry, chapped skin, reducing inflammation and promoting moisture retention.
Vitamins a c e in GLYCRRHIZA GLABRA contain properties that help to moisturize and soothe dry lips by locking in moisture, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture is a natural remedy for dry, chapped lips. It's made from the gel of the aloe plant, which is rich in vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids. These nutrients help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin. When applied to dry lips, the aloe tincture can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort. It also has antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infection and speed up the healing process. Additionally, aloe tincture is gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for sensitive skin. It can be applied directly to the lips, and can be used as often as needed to keep them moisturized and healthy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture for dry lips, start by preparing the roots of the licorice plant. You can purchase dried licorice root from a health food store or online. Clean and chop the root into small pieces, then place them in a clean glass jar. Next, fill a separate glass jar with a neutral-tasting liquid such as vodka or glycerin, making sure the liquid covers the roots completely. Store the jars in a cool, dark place and let them sit for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two, to allow the roots to infuse their properties into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your licorice root tincture, which can be used to help soothe and moisturize dry lips. To use, simply apply a few drops of the tincture to your lips with a cotton swab, and reapply as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe and heal dry lips. The plant, also known as comfrey, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling. When applied to dry lips, the tincture helps lock in moisture and protect the skin from the dry air. This is because comfrey contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the skin, making it an effective solution for chapped and cracked lips. Additionally, the tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent infections that can make dry lips even worse. As a result, using Symphytum officinale tincture regularly can help keep your lips hydrated, soft, and healthy. When used in combination with a lip balm or moisturizer, it can provide extra protection and nourishment to dry lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with dry lips, you will need fresh or dried licorice root, a clean glass jar, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a dark glass bottle for storing the tincture. First, prepare the licorice root by washing and drying it. If using fresh root, chop it into small pieces and dry them in the oven at a low temperature or by air-drying. If using dried root, simply crush it into smaller pieces. Next, place the licorice root pieces in the glass jar and pour the solvent over them, making sure that the root is completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the solvent extract the medicinal properties from the root. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, and discard the solids. The resulting tincture can be used to help soothe and moisturize dry lips by applying a few drops to the affected area several times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures are good for dry lips because they have healing properties that help to soothe and moisturize the skin. When you apply aloe vera tincture to dry lips, it quickly absorbs into the skin, providing relief from the dryness and discomfort. The tincture contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help to nourish the skin and promote healthy cell growth. Aloe vera also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective remedy for chapped and cracked lips. Additionally, aloe vera tincture can help to lock in moisture, keeping your lips hydrated and soft for a longer period of time.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture for dry lips, you will need dried licorice root, a clean glass jar, vodka, and a dropper bottle. First, fill the glass jar with about an ounce of dried licorice root. Then, fill the jar with vodka, making sure the root is completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This will help the vodka extract the active properties from the licorice root. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dropper bottle, discarding the solids. Your tincture is now ready to use. To apply it to your dry lips, simply add a few drops to your lips and gently massage it in. You can also mix it with a little bit of water or oil to create a lip balm. Note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe and heal dry, chapped lips. It contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling, making it a great option for people with sensitive skin. The tincture's soothing properties can calm irritation and discomfort, allowing the skin to heal quickly. Chamomilla recutita is also known to have moisturizing properties, which can help lock in moisture and protect the skin from further dryness. This can be especially helpful during the winter months or in dry environments where lips tend to become dry and cracked. When applied to dry lips, the tincture's anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties can work together to provide relief and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture for dry lips, you'll need dried licorice root, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or glycerin as a solvent, and a dark glass bottle for storage. First, fill the glass jar with about 1 cup of dried licorice root, making sure it's completely covered with the vodka or glycerin. Secure the lid tightly, then place the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every day. This process allows the licorice root to extract its active compounds into the solvent. After the incubation period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. You can now use this tincture to help soothe dry lips by applying a few drops to the affected area with a cotton swab, ideally after meals and before bedtime. Always dilute the tincture with water or another carrier oil before applying it to your skin, as it can be quite strong.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a natural remedy for dry lips. The plant's roots and flowers contain special compounds that help soothe and moisturize skin. When applied to dry lips, these compounds reduce inflammation and irritation, making lips feel softer and more comfortable. Echinacea purpurea tincture also has antiseptic properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections and make dry lips worse. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to heal dry and chapped lips. By applying Echinacea purpurea tincture to dry lips, you can help lock in moisture and protect your lips from harsh environmental conditions, such as wind and cold weather. This natural remedy can be used as a preventative measure to keep your lips healthy and hydrated throughout the year.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with dry lips, start by gathering dried licorice root, a glass jar with a lid, a cheesecloth or coffee filter, and a carrier oil like olive or grapeseed oil. Fill the glass jar with 2-3 ounces of dried licorice root, making sure it's completely covered with the carrier oil. Secure the lid tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the oil extract the active compounds from the licorice root. After the extraction period, use the cheesecloth or coffee filter to strain the liquid into another glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use a few drops as needed to soothe dry, chapped lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tincture, also known as peppermint tincture, can help soothe and heal dry lips. The menthol in peppermint cools the skin, providing instant relief from the dryness and discomfort. When applied to dry lips, the tincture helps to lock in moisture and speed up the healing process. The antibacterial properties of peppermint also prevent infections that can make dry lips worse. Peppermint tincture is a natural and easy way to care for dry lips, and it can be applied as needed to keep lips soft and healthy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture that helps with dry lips, start by gathering the necessary ingredients and equipment. You will need dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, a measuring cup, a dropper, and a solvent such as vodka or glycerin. Begin by measuring one part of dried roots to two parts of solvent in the glass jar, and make sure the roots are completely covered by the solvent. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to help the roots release their active ingredients into the solvent. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, and discard the solids. You can now use the resulting tincture by applying a few drops to your dry lips as needed, or by mixing it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for a lip balm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a type of herbal remedy that can help soothe dry, chapped lips. The tincture is made from the leaves of the lemon balm plant, which contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm and moisturize the skin. When applied to dry lips, the Melissa officinalis tincture can help lock in moisture and reduce irritation. It can also help to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, leaving lips feeling smoother and softer. Additionally, lemon balm has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote healing in dry, cracked skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for dry lips, start by drying the roots of the licorice plant, which is the source of glycyrrhizin, an active compound that helps soothe and moisturize dry skin. Once the roots are dry, chop them into small pieces and combine them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. The general ratio is one part dried licorice root to three parts carrier oil. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the licorice root infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This liquid tincture can be applied directly to the lips to help lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is a natural remedy for dry, chapped lips. The main active ingredient, glycyrrhizin, is a powerful anti-inflammatory that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin. It also has moisturizing properties that lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness. When applied to dry lips, the tincture helps to repair the skin's natural barrier, allowing it to retain moisture and stay hydrated. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections and promote healing. The tincture is also easy to apply, simply by adding a few drops to a lip balm or applying it directly to the lips. Its natural ingredients make it a safe and gentle option for people with sensitive skin, and it can be used as often as needed to keep lips hydrated and healthy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture for dry lips, you will need dried licorice root, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a dropper. Start by measuring out one part of dried licorice root and adding it to the glass jar, then fill the jar with two parts of the vodka, making sure that the licorice root is completely covered. Seal the jar with the lid and place it in a cool, dark place, allowing it to steep for two weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This will allow the glycyrrhizin, a compound in the licorice root with anti-inflammatory properties, to infuse into the vodka. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the licorice root. The tincture is now ready to use, simply place a few drops on your dry lips and let the glycyrrhizin work to soothe and moisturize them.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for dry lips

intro_desc: Dry, chapped lips can be painful and unsightly. They can also be a sign of an underlying issue, such as dehydration or a skin condition. Herbal tinctures can provide relief and help to heal dry lips. Two herbs that are effective in treating dry lips are Calendula officinalis and Aloe barbadensis. Calendula, also known as marigold, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin. Aloe vera, from the Aloe barbadensis plant, is known for its moisturizing and soothing properties, which can help to hydrate and soften dry lips. By using a tincture made from these herbs, you can help to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and lock in moisture to keep your lips soft and healthy.  It's essential to address dry lips because they can be a source of discomfort and self-consciousness. Dry, chapped lips can make everyday activities, such as eating, talking, or even smiling, more difficult. By treating dry lips, you can feel more confident and comfortable in your own skin. Moreover, taking care of your skin and mouth can also have a positive impact on your overall health and well-being. By prioritizing the health of your skin and lips, you can improve your quality of life and reduce the risk of more serious skin conditions.
supplementary_best: For dry lips, a combination of Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, and Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures is beneficial. Aloe barbadensis tincture helps to soothe and moisturize the lips, reducing inflammation and providing a cooling sensation. Calendula officinalis tincture adds anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in healing and protecting the lips from infections. Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture, containing licorice root, adds a soothing and moisturizing effect, while also providing antioxidant properties to shield the lips from damage caused by free radicals. This combination creates a synergistic effect, allowing the tinctures to work together to provide long-lasting relief from dry lips.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry lips, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. One of these is tincture of Sassafras (Sassafras albidum), which can be irritating to the skin. Sassafras contains compounds that can cause allergic reactions and may make dry lips worse. Another tincture to avoid is Wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens), which contains a compound called methyl salicylate. This can be too harsh for dry skin and may cause further irritation. Also, avoid tincture of Witch Hazel (Hamamelis virginiana), as its astringent properties can dry out the skin even more, making dry lips worse. Lastly, tincture of Turmeric (Curcuma longa) can be too harsh for dry skin, as it contains curcumin, a compound that can cause skin irritation in some people.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help soothe dry lips. It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce irritation and redness. When applied to the lips, aloe vera gel provides a protective barrier, locking in moisture and preventing water loss. This helps to hydrate and soften the lips, making them feel smooth and comfortable.  Beeswax is another natural ingredient that can help with dry lips. It creates a protective barrier on the surface of the lips, preventing moisture from escaping. This helps to lock in moisture and keep the lips hydrated. Beeswax can be found in many lip balms and products, and is often combined with other natural ingredients like coconut oil or shea butter.  Coconut oil is a natural moisturizer that can help hydrate dry lips. It contains fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and soothe irritation. When applied to the lips, coconut oil can help to soften and smooth out dry, cracked skin. It's also easy to apply and can be used as a standalone product or added to other natural remedies for extra moisturizing benefits.  Shea butter is a natural moisturizer that can help hydrate dry lips. It contains vitamins and fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and soothe irritation. When applied to the lips, shea butter can help to soften and smooth out dry, cracked skin. It's also rich in antioxidants that can help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage.  Honey is a natural humectant that can help retain moisture in the lips. It's sticky and sweet, and can help to attract and lock in moisture. When applied to the lips, honey can help to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin. It's also antibacterial and antifungal, making it a great natural remedy for preventing infections and promoting healing.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dry lips often experience other issues due to the same underlying causes, such as dehydration and dry skin. They may develop cracked skin on their hands and feet, as the skin's natural moisture barrier is disrupted. This can be treated with medicinal herbs like aloe vera, which helps to lock in moisture and soothe irritated skin. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can also reduce redness and swelling in the affected areas.  Dry lips can also be a sign of an underlying condition, such as eczema or psoriasis. In these cases, people may experience itchy, scaly patches on other parts of their body, such as their elbows, knees, or scalp. Medicinal herbs like chamomile and calendula can help to calm and soothe these skin conditions, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  Some people with dry lips may also experience digestive issues, such as constipation or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). This can be due to a lack of fiber or moisture in the diet, leading to dehydration and dryness in the digestive tract. Medicinal herbs like slippery elm and marshmallow root can help to soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy bowel movements.  Additionally, people with dry lips may experience respiratory issues, such as dry, scratchy throats or congestion. This can be due to a lack of moisture in the air, leading to dehydration in the respiratory tract. Medicinal herbs like thyme and eucalyptus can help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy mucus production.
